stage.name: Forwarding: bypassing a wait without lying
stage.description: A forwarding lesson explaining functional-unit result forwarding, scoreboard-entry forwarding, non-exception requirement, CSR limitation, and operand replacement before EX_STAGE.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

content.title: Forwarding: bypassing a wait without lying
content.subtitle: Forwarding is not ignoring a dependency; it is proving the needed value is already available through a legal path.
content.thesis: Forwarding lets ISSUE_STAGE avoid some RAW stalls only when the replacement operand is real, matched, and safe.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

content.speakerNotes: Forwarding is the part of hazard logic that feels like a shortcut, but it is not cheating. It does not ignore a dependency. It proves that the needed value is already available from a legal source, then replaces the default register-file operand before the instruction goes to execute.

actions.text: Forwarding is the part of hazard logic that feels like a shortcut, but it is not cheating. It does not ignore a dependency. It proves that the needed value is already available from a legal source, then replaces the default register-file operand before the instruction goes to execute.

content.title: Forward from a returning result or a ready scoreboard entry
content.subtitle: The source describes two paths that can satisfy a RAW dependence.

columns.title: Functional-unit return
columns.body: A functional unit returns a non-exception result with a transaction ID pointing to the matching scoreboard entry.

columns.title: Scoreboard entry
columns.body: A scoreboard entry whose destination matches the source register already contains a non-exception result.

content.takeaway: Forwarding needs both identity and validity: the right producer and a safe value.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Scoreboard submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The scoreboard contains a FIFO of issued instructions, has capacity for four entries in this configuration, stores returned results by transaction ID, and exposes oldest completed entries to commit.

content.speakerNotes: The source gives two forwarding sources. First, a functional unit may return a non-exception result with a transaction ID pointing to the scoreboard entry whose destination matches the needed source. Second, the scoreboard entry may already contain a non-exception result. In both cases, identity and validity matter. Wrong producer or exception result means no safe forwarding.

actions.text: The source gives two forwarding sources. First, a functional unit may return a non-exception result with a transaction ID pointing to the scoreboard entry whose destination matches the needed source. Second, the scoreboard entry may already contain a non-exception result. In both cases, identity and validity matter. Wrong producer or exception result means no safe forwarding.

scenes.title: Register file by default, forwarded value when proven

content.title: How operands are chosen
content.subtitle: Issue_read_operands provides EX_STAGE input operands from the register file unless forwarding overrides them.

steps.title: Read default operands
steps.body: The register file supplies source values by default.

steps.title: Check RAW dependence
steps.body: If a source matches an older destination, issue logic searches forwarding evidence.

steps.title: Replace operand
steps.body: If forwarding is legal, the input operand is replaced by the forwarded value.

steps.title: Register into EX_STAGE
steps.body: Issued instructions are visible in EX_STAGE one cycle after issue.

content.takeaway: Forwarding changes the operand source, not the architectural meaning of the instruction.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Issue_read_operands submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Issue_read_operands tracks hazards, obtains input operands, uses register file values by default, can replace operands with forwarded values, and sends issued instructions to EX_STAGE through a register.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

content.speakerNotes: The operand path is clean. The register file provides values by default. If a RAW dependence appears, Issue_read_operands looks for forwarding evidence. When the evidence is legal, it replaces the operand with the forwarded value. Then the instruction is sent to EX_STAGE through a register and becomes visible there one cycle after issue. That is a concrete timing story, not just a magic arrow.

actions.text: The operand path is clean. The register file provides values by default. If a RAW dependence appears, Issue_read_operands looks for forwarding evidence. When the evidence is legal, it replaces the operand with the forwarded value. Then the instruction is sent to EX_STAGE through a register and becomes visible there one cycle after issue. That is a concrete timing story, not just a magic arrow.

scenes.title: Forwarding is a loop through tracked state

content.title: The scoreboard makes forwarding inspectable
content.subtitle: Forwarding is understandable once you see the result buses, transaction IDs, and scoreboard entries as one loop.
content.body: A forwarded operand must correspond to a tracked producer. That is why the scoreboard, forwarding bundle, and issue operand logic are part of the same story.
content.caption: Use the scoreboard diagram to follow forwarding evidence.

callouts.title: Consumer
callouts.body: New source operand gets replacement value.

content.takeaway: Forwarding is easiest to trust when you can name producer, evidence, and consumer.

assets.caption: Official scoreboard diagram used to explain issue, hazards, forwarding, and result tracking.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Scoreboard submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The scoreboard contains a FIFO of issued instructions, has capacity for four entries in this configuration, stores returned results by transaction ID, and exposes oldest completed entries to commit.

content.speakerNotes: Use the diagram to follow the forwarding loop. There is an older producer, a piece of evidence that its result is ready and not an exception, and a younger consumer whose source operand can use that value. If you cannot name those three things, do not call it forwarding yet. You might just be wishing really hard, which remains a poor microarchitecture technique.

actions.text: Use the diagram to follow the forwarding loop. There is an older producer, a piece of evidence that its result is ready and not an exception, and a younger consumer whose source operand can use that value. If you cannot name those three things, do not call it forwarding yet. You might just be wishing really hard, which remains a poor microarchitecture technique.

content.title: Four things that stop a forwarding claim
content.subtitle: The public course should be explicit about the caveats.

items.title: Exception result
items.body: Forwarding requires a result that is not an exception.

items.title: Wrong transaction ID
items.body: The returning result must point to the matching scoreboard entry.

items.title: CSR instruction
items.body: The source says forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

items.title: No ready result
items.body: If the scoreboard entry has no ready value, issue must still wait.

content.takeaway: Forwarding is a permissioned bypass, not a universal hazard eraser.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Issue_read_operands submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Issue_read_operands tracks hazards, obtains input operands, uses register file values by default, can replace operands with forwarded values, and sends issued instructions to EX_STAGE through a register.

content.speakerNotes: Forwarding has limits. Exception results do not count. The transaction ID has to identify the right producer. CSR instructions cannot be forwarded from, according to the source. And if the scoreboard entry has no ready result yet, there is nothing to forward. This is why forwarding is a permissioned bypass, not a universal hazard eraser.

actions.text: Forwarding has limits. Exception results do not count. The transaction ID has to identify the right producer. CSR instructions cannot be forwarded from, according to the source. And if the scoreboard entry has no ready result yet, there is nothing to forward. This is why forwarding is a permissioned bypass, not a universal hazard eraser.

questions.question: What does forwarding do in this lesson?

options.label: It replaces a default register-file operand with a safe forwarded value when RAW evidence allows it
It flushes the frontend on every branch
It turns WAW hazards into cache misses

questions.analysis: Forwarding supplies a replacement operand for RAW dependencies when the value is already safely available.

questions.question: Which sources can provide a forwarded operand according to the source?

options.label: A non-exception functional-unit result with the matching transaction ID
A scoreboard entry with a matching destination register and ready non-exception result
A random younger instruction that has not issued

questions.analysis: Forwarding comes from returning FU results or ready scoreboard entries, not unrelated younger work or branch target structures.

questions.question: Which limitation is explicitly stated for forwarding?

options.label: Forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions
Forwarding requires the BTB to be present

questions.analysis: The source explicitly states that forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Issue_read_operands submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Issue_read_operands tracks hazards, obtains input operands, uses register file values by default, can replace operands with forwarded values, and sends issued instructions to EX_STAGE through a register.

content.speakerNotes: This checkpoint should make forwarding feel less mystical. It is a legal operand substitution backed by a tracked producer and a ready non-exception value. If that evidence is missing, the issue stage must wait. Bypass without proof is just a bug with confidence.

actions.text: This checkpoint should make forwarding feel less mystical. It is a legal operand substitution backed by a tracked producer and a ready non-exception value. If that evidence is missing, the issue stage must wait. Bypass without proof is just a bug with confidence.

content.title: Some RAW hazards can move, but only with evidence
content.subtitle: Forwarding preserves correctness by replacing the operand only when the right result is already available.
takeaways: Default operands come from the register file.
Forwarding can use returning FU results or ready scoreboard entries.
CSR, exception, and missing-result cases still block forwarding.
content.nextStep: Next: structural hazards, where the operand may be ready but the execution resource or result bus is not.
content.closing: Forwarding is the backend saying: I do not need to wait, because I can prove the value is here.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: operand forwarding
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: Forwarding can take operands from a returning functional-unit result or from a scoreboard entry with a ready non-exception result; forwarding is not possible from CSR instructions.

content.speakerNotes: Forwarding is controlled optimism. It lets a RAW-dependent instruction move only when the correct value is already available through a legal path. Next we study structural hazards. That is a different failure mode: the data may be ready, but the functional unit or result bus may not be.

actions.text: Forwarding is controlled optimism. It lets a RAW-dependent instruction move only when the correct value is already available through a legal path. Next we study structural hazards. That is a different failure mode: the data may be ready, but the functional unit or result bus may not be.
